channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: The 5 Principles Behind Agent-Native Software Architecture
item.description: Most AI applications are chatbots wearing a thin veneer of features. The agent is an afterthought — a chat interface bolted onto existing code. Users ask it to do things. It apologizes and...

item.title: The itgps-agent: Bringing Studio-Managed Configs to Your Local Workflow
item.description: The itgps-agent: Bringing Studio-Managed Configs to Your Local Workflow In Part 1 and Part 2, we explored ITG Playwright Studio's web interface for managing tests. But what if you're a developer...

item.title: A Visual Tour of ITG Playwright Studio: Managing Tests Through a Web Interface
item.description: A Visual Tour of ITG Playwright Studio In Part 1, we discussed why managing Playwright tests at scale is challenging and introduced ITG Playwright Studio as a solution. Now let's take a detailed...

item.title: Playwright is Powerful, But Managing It at Scale? That's Another Story
item.description: Playwright is Powerful, But Managing It at Scale? That's Another Story If you've worked with Playwright, you know it's an incredible tool for end-to-end testing. The API is clean, the...

item.title: Building an MCP server — lessons from thunderbit-mcp
item.description: When we started building thunderbit-mcp, the plan sounded straightforward: expose Thunderbit's web extraction API to AI coding agents through the Model Context Protocol. In practice, the hard...

item.title: Four Pillars, One Platform: How Cybrium Unifies Code, Cloud, AI, and GRC
item.description: A friend of mine runs security at a 200-engineer SaaS company. Last winter she got paged at 2 a.m. for an exposed S3 bucket. Customer PII. The bucket had been flagged by their cloud scanner three...

item.title: 67. DBSCAN: Clustering That Handles Messy Data
item.description: Last post K-Means failed on crescent-shaped data. It cut across the natural curves instead of following them. You also had to tell it K upfront. And one outlier could drag a centroid completely...

item.title: OpenCode Integration Practice: Architectural Evolution from Standalone Process to Shared Runtime
item.description: OpenCode Integration Practice: Architectural Evolution from Standalone Process to Shared Runtime This article shares the complete practice of HagiCode integrating the OpenCode AI assistant,...

item.title: Created a agent friendly API directory https://findapi.dev/
item.description: API Directory for Developers | Find & Compare Public APIs Explore a searchable API directory for developers. Find and compare public APIs by category, pricing, authentication, and use case....

item.title: What Does "Building in Public" Actually Mean in 2026?
item.description: If you've spent any time on developer Twitter, you've seen it. Founders posting their MRR. Sharing what broke this week. Documenting every step of building their product in real time. But what...

item.title: Get ready for the whisper-filled office of the future
item.description: How will work setups change if we spend more and more time talking to our computers?

item.title: The Bastl Kalimba is a wild synth that thinks it’s a thumb piano
item.description: Make no mistake, the Bastl Kalimba is a synthesizer, you just play it like a kalimba. Its tines don't really make much sound. There is an internal mic that you can blend in for a little acoustic...

item.title: Anthropic says ‘evil’ portrayals of AI were responsible for Claude’s blackmail attempts
item.description: Fictional portrayals of artificial intelligence can have a real effect on AI models, according to Anthropic.

item.title: MachinaCheck: Building a Multi-Agent CNC Manufacturability System on AMD MI300X
Ashnymph’s Childhood EP is exhilarating dance goth rock
item.description: I've got to thank my oldest friend and concert buddy, Tim, for turning me on to this one. Ashnymph is a London band that blends post-punk melodies with Krautrock rhythms and industrial grime....

item.title: We’re feeling cynical about xAI’s big deal with Anthropic
item.description: On the latest episode of the Equity podcast, we discussed what xAI's deal with Anthropic might mean for parent company SpaceX.

item.title: Cricut’s $99 craft cutting machine helped me feel creative again
item.description: I've always been skeptical of products that claim to help you lead a more creative life. But one recently won me over. I've spent three weeks with the Cricut Joy 2, a smart cutting and drawing...

item.title: Writers are fleeing the Substack Tax
item.description: Substack, the once buzzy newsletter platform, is losing a new swath of writers to rival platforms most people haven't heard of. Just last month, The Ankler, one of Substack's most popular...

item.title: Ollama Out-of-Bounds Read Vulnerability Allows Remote Process Memory Leak
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a critical security vulnerability in Ollama that, if successfully exploited, could allow a remote, unauthenticated attacker to leak its entire process...

item.title: Netflix may have finally figured out games
item.description: This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more on Netflix, follow Andrew Webster. The Stepback arrives in our subscribers' inboxes at 8AM...

item.title: Vivo’s X300 Ultra has the best cameras in any phone
item.description: A few months ago, I wrote that the telephoto camera is the only lens that matters any more, at least when it comes to Ultra-class flagships. As phones got better, cameras became where...

item.title: Voice AI in India is hard. Wispr Flow is betting on it anyway.
item.description: Wispr Flow says growth accelerated in India after its Hinglish rollout, even as voice AI products continue to face challenges.

item.title: So you’ve heard these AI terms and nodded along; let’s fix that
item.description: The rise of AI has brought an avalanche of new terms and slang. Here is a glossary with definitions of some of the most important words and phrases you might encounter.

item.title: Dyson’s powerful 360 Vis Nav robovac is down to $279.99 for a limited time
item.description: If you’re tired of running your vacuum multiple times just to get the dirt and debris out of the carpets in your living room, Dyson’s 360 Vis Nav is worth a look. It’s one of the more powerful...

item.title: Nvidia has already committed $40B to equity AI deals this year
item.description: Nvidia continues to be a big investor in the AI ecosystem.

item.title: These great digital gifts will arrive just in time for Mother’s Day
item.description: And just like that, Mother’s Day is tomorrow, May 10th, which is too soon for most online purchases to arrive in time. That said, you aren’t alone if you waited too long to pick up a gift this...

item.title: Let it snow
item.description: Water gets all the credit. When gaming companies want to show off new graphics technology, things tend to get wet; splashing waves that are only possible with the latest physics engine, or puddles...

item.title: The game that makes me actually want to exercise
item.description: Hi, friends! Welcome to Installer No. 127, your guide to the best and Verge-iest stuff in the world. (If you're new here, welcome, my Scorpion challenges your Sub-Zero to a duel, and also you can...

item.title: Hackable Robot Lawn Mower Unlocks a New Nightmare
item.description: Plus: Meta officially kills encrypted Instagram DMs, the Trump administration targets “violent left wing extremists,” leaked documents reveal Russia's school for elite hackers, and more.

item.title: cPanel, WHM Release Fixes for Three New Vulnerabilities — Patch Now
item.description: cPanel has released updates to address three vulnerabilities in cPanel and Web Host Manager (WHM) that could be exploited to achieve privilege escalation, code execution, and denial-of-service....
